# Standards — META domain

<!-- Auto-generated by JOS charter sync. DO NOT EDIT. -->
<!-- Generated at: 2026-07-17T13:20:29.663467+00:00 -->

This index lists every JOS-canonical standard declared under the `meta` domain. Source of truth: jordan-operating-system repo.

**Count:** 43

| ID | Title | Stage | Flexibility | Summary |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [JOS-S01](JOS-S01-discoverable-registries.md) | Discoverable Registries | adopt | expected | Define the standard pattern for making every resource category discoverable and scoped. |
| [JOS-S02](JOS-S02-universal-placement.md) | Universal Placement | adopt | expected | Define the standard for ensuring every artifact has a classified home in the taxonomy. |
| [JOS-S04](JOS-S04-creation-automation-tiers.md) | Creation Automation Tiers | adopt | suggested | Define the standard maturity tiers for automating creation workflows. |
| [JOS-S05](JOS-S05-governance-object-lifecycle.md) | Governance Object Lifecycle | adopt | expected | Define the standard lifecycle stages for governance objects. |
| [JOS-S06](JOS-S06-review-cadence.md) | Review Cadence | adopt | expected | Define the standard for periodic review of all governed components. |
| [JOS-S07](JOS-S07-all-governed-objects-registered.md) | All Governed Components Must Be Registered | adopt | required | Without explicit registry coverage of delivery-layer objects (SOPs, playbooks, gates, templates, monitors, specs), the registry only tells half the truth. |
| [JOS-S08](JOS-S08-catalogue-standard.md) | Domain-Scoped Venture Catalogues | adopt | expected | Ventures need to track things that matter — technologies, vendors, processes, team structures — without the overhead of full governance. |
| [JOS-S15](JOS-S15-feature-registry-standard.md) | Feature Registry Standard | trial | expected | Features exist as informal bookkeeping in features.yaml files with no governed schema, no validation, no staleness model, and no clear promotion path to capabilities. |
| [JOS-S16](JOS-S16-governance-discovery-standard.md) | Governance Discovery Standard | trial | expected | Define what good looks like for governance discovery — the ability of agents and humans to find any governed component, feature, term, or capability within 30 seconds using documented paths. |
| [JOS-S17](JOS-S17-quality-triad-venture-standard.md) | Quality Triad Venture Standard | trial | expected | Define how ventures implement the Quality Triad — the three complementary quality dimensions (Definition, Operation, Performance) that every governed entity must address. |
| [JOS-S18](JOS-S18-naming-and-identity-standard.md) | Naming and Identity Standard | trial | expected | Governed artifacts need predictable, discoverable names. |
| [JOS-S31](JOS-S31-id-naming-convention.md) | ID Naming Convention Standard | trial | required | JOS-S18 says "names should be discoverable" at the file level. |
| [JOS-S32](JOS-S32-workbench-doc-standard.md) | WORKBENCH Doc Standard | adopt | expected | Every venture has a `WORKBENCH/` directory by default; today they're authored ad-hoc — different folder shapes, no frontmatter, no promotion-to-canonical playbook. |
| [JOS-S33](JOS-S33-outbox-discipline-standard.md) | Outbox Discipline Standard | adopt | required | Without a discipline standard, ventures may author substrate candidates (rules, standards, principles, etc.) directly into their .jos/ canonical directories, bypassing JOS triage. |
| [JOS-S34](JOS-S34-knowledge-document-schema.md) | Knowledge Document Schema | adopt | required | JOS knowledge bases (Project Silk's 10-domain library, Rocky Ridge's ingested corpus, Ecomlever's Wayward content, future ventures) need a single canonical frontmatter schema so Foundry CIP can inges… |
| [JOS-S35](JOS-S35-knowledge-organization.md) | Knowledge Organization Standard | adopt | expected | JOS S34 locks the per-document schema; S35 (this standard) locks how documents organize into a venture's knowledge base. |
| [JOS-S37](JOS-S37-standard-doc-standard.md) | Standard Doc-Standard (meta-recursion closure) | trial | expected | Every Standard instance follows a consistent author voice + section shape + frontmatter cascade. |
| [JOS-S38](JOS-S38-decision-doc-standard.md) | Decision Doc-Standard | trial | expected | 79 Decisions exist in JOS canon. |
| [JOS-S39](JOS-S39-method-doc-standard.md) | Method Doc-Standard | trial | expected | 30 Methods exist (29 active + 1 SOP-014 redirect). |
| [JOS-S40](JOS-S40-concept-doc-standard.md) | Concept Doc-Standard | trial | expected | 52 Concepts exist in JOS canon. |
| [JOS-S41](JOS-S41-rule-doc-standard.md) | Rule Doc-Standard | trial | expected | 19 Rules exist. |
| [JOS-S42](JOS-S42-principle-doc-standard.md) | Principle Doc-Standard | trial | expected | 24 Principles exist. |

| [JOS-S47](JOS-S47-sop-doc-standard.md) | SOP Doc-Standard | trial | expected | 17 SOPs exist. |
| [JOS-S48](JOS-S48-playbook-doc-standard.md) | Playbook Doc-Standard | trial | expected | 7 Playbooks exist. |
| [JOS-S49](JOS-S49-diagnostic-doc-standard.md) | Diagnostic Doc-Standard | trial | expected | 2 Diagnostics exist — under-covered substrate type. |

| [JOS-S51](JOS-S51-directory-doc-standard.md) | Directory Doc-Standard | trial | expected | 6 Directories exist (newly authored 2026-05-20). |
| [JOS-S52](JOS-S52-component-doc-standard.md) | Component Doc-Standard | adopt | expected | JOS-D0085 ratified Component as new first-class substrate; JOS-SPEC-024 (v1.0–v1.3) locks the YAML schema. |
| [JOS-S54](JOS-S54-task-definition-doc-standard.md) | Task Definition Doc-Standard | trial | expected | Task Definitions are the I/O-contract substrate — they specify what inputs a unit of work takes, what outputs it produces, and how to verify the work was done correctly. |
| [JOS-S55](JOS-S55-project-template-doc-standard.md) | Project Template Doc-Standard | trial | expected | Project Templates compose other Library substrate — they declare a skeleton of scopes, name suggested task definitions, reference related schemas / playbooks / SOPs. |
| [JOS-S56](JOS-S56-slot-system-standard.md) | Slot System Standard | trial | expected | Library Templates accept parameterized inputs ("slots") that get resolved at instantiate-time from one of several declared sources (human, default, prior_context, fkm_lookup, agent_inference, tool:*). |
| [JOS-S57](JOS-S57-role-charter-doc-standard.md) | Role Charter Doc-Standard | trial | expected | A role (seat) needs a job description before it is filled — agent or human. |
| [JOS-S58](JOS-S58-onboarding-roadmap-doc-standard.md) | Onboarding Roadmap Doc-Standard | trial | expected | A role is not finished at birth (JOS-P29): real competence comes from the ramp — probing the seat, fixing what breaks, acquiring the tools it turns out to need, and climbing the trust ladder. |
| [JOS-S59](JOS-S59-role-archetype-standard.md) | Role Archetype Standard | trial | expected | Roles cluster into families — Auditor, Analyst, Coordinator, Specialist, Warden — and JOS-P29 commits to composing roles from a first-class archetype library rather than designing each bespoke. |
| [JOS-S60](JOS-S60-sop-research-standard.md) | SOP Research Standard | trial | expected | JOS-P28 commits to researching a role's SOPs, skills, and tools against the world standard before the role runs — turning "the best human in the world would do X" from a guess into an evidence-backed… |
| [JOS-S61](JOS-S61-work-packet-completeness-standard.md) | Work-Packet Completeness Standard | trial | required | JOS-SPEC-027 defines the SHAPE of a Work Packet, but shape-present is not the same as complete-and-fit-to-operate. |
| [JOS-S62](JOS-S62-scratchpad-discipline-standard.md) | Scratchpad Discipline Standard | trial | expected | JOS-R20 requires a scratchpad directory but a bare requirement leaves every repo inventing its own shape. |
| [JOS-S63](JOS-S63-agent-conduct-rulebook-standard.md) | Agent Conduct Rulebook Standard | trial | expected | Agent CONDUCT — how any agent of a given type behaves, independent of its particular job — had no governed home. |
| [JOS-S64](JOS-S64-question-authoring-standard.md) | Question Authoring Standard | trial | expected | Questions as cognition (JOS-C54) only helps if the questions are well-made. |
